extraction.one_line_summary: SAGAN Peter won a reduced sprint at Foligno after BORA-hansgrohe's pace-making from 43km shed multiple sprinters including NIZZOLO Giacomo.
tactical_patterns: BORA-hansgrohe tempo from 43km eliminated pure sprinters before sprint trains could organize, NIZZOLO dropped despite GANNA pacing back.
EVENEPOEL Remco and NARVÁEZ Jhonatan contested intermediate sprint for bonus seconds, BERNAL Egan took 1 second indicating GC teams prioritizing margins.
Crash inside final 3km neutralized GC group time gaps, rewarding teams willing to keep leaders forward before sprint chaos.
MOLANO Juan Sebastián led out but SAGAN stayed third wheel through corner, suggesting experience trumps pure speed in reduced finishes.

rider_signals.SAGAN Peter: positive: won after extended BORA tempo eliminated pure sprinters, suggests he thrives when field is reduced pre-finale
rider_signals.NIZZOLO Giacomo: negative: dropped during BORA pace-making despite GANNA assistance, vulnerable when sustained tempo precedes sprint
rider_signals.GAVIRIA Fernando: negative: beaten in reduced sprint despite surviving BORA pace, suggests limited effectiveness without leadout train
rider_signals.EVENEPOEL Remco: positive: actively contesting intermediate sprint bonus seconds at 14 seconds down, showing aggressive GC racing mentality
rider_signals.BERNAL Egan: neutral: took 1 bonus second at intermediate sprint, defending conservatively but alert to time gains
rider_signals.CIMOLAI Davide: neutral: third place in reduced sprint but still seeking first Giro stage win, consistent without breakthrough
rider_signals.DEKKER David: negative: dropped during BORA pace-making, unable to survive extended high tempo before sprint
rider_signals.VIVIANI Elia: negative: barely top 10 in reduced sprint, major disappointment compared to expected sprint performance
